2604.14053.title: From Where Words Come: Efficient Regularization of Code Tokenizers Through Source Attribution
2604.14053.paper_summary: Efficiency and safety of Large Language Models (LLMs), among other factors, rely on the quality of tokenization. A good tokenizer not only improves inference speed and language understanding but also provides extra defense against jailbreak attacks and lowers the risk of hallucinations. In this work, we investigate the efficiency of code tokenization, in particular from the perspective of data source diversity. We demonstrate that code tokenizers are prone to producing unused, and thus under-trained, tokens due to the imbalance in repository and language diversity in the training data, as well as the dominance of source-specific, repetitive tokens that are often unusable in future inference. By modifying the BPE objective and introducing merge skipping, we implement different techniques under the name Source-Attributed BPE (SA-BPE) to regularize BPE training and minimize overfitting, thereby substantially reducing the number of under-trained tokens while maintaining the same inference procedure as with regular BPE. This provides an effective tool suitable for production use.
2604.14053.paper_summary_zh: 效率和安全性，大型語言模型（LLMs）等因素，依賴於標記化的質量。<lb/>一個好的標記器不僅提高推理速度和語言理解，還提供額外的防禦以抵抗越獄攻擊，並降低幻覺的風險。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們研究了代碼標記化的效率，特別是從數據來源多樣性的角度。<lb/>我們證明，由於訓練數據中庫和語言多樣性的不平衡，以及源特定的重複標記的主導地位，代碼標記器容易產生未使用的、因此未經充分訓練的標記，這些標記在未來的推理中往往是無法使用的。<lb/>通過修改BPE目標並引入合併跳過，我們以源屬性BPE（SA-BPE）的名義實施不同的技術，以規範BPE訓練並最小化過擬合，從而在保持與常規BPE相同的推理過程的同時，顯著減少未經充分訓練的標記數量。<lb/>這提供了一個適合生產使用的有效工具。
2604.14053.author: Pavel Chizhov et.al.
2604.14053.authors: Pavel Chizhov, Egor Bogomolov, Ivan P. Yamshchikov
